content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


September 16, 2002


Debra A. Benci-Woodward
Post Office Box 635
Ojai, CA 93024

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-243

Dear Ms. Benci-Woodward:

This letter confirms the receipt of your August 28, 2002 request for advice regarding an unnamed council member. Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized representative of the council member for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your question or provide analysis. I enclose regulations 18704.2, 18705.2 and our pamphlet, "Can I Vote? Conflict of Interest Overview" for your examination.

Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosures

sections.conclusion: Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), the FPPC cannot address the question or provide analysis unless the requestor is an authorized representative of the council member.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC can provide advice regarding an unnamed council member's potential conflict of interest or ethics issue.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the requestor is not an authorized representative of the unnamed council member, which is required under applicable regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no substantive QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; regulatory requirement cited as basis for declining advice. File number given as W-02-243.

QUESTION: Whether the FPPC can provide advice regarding an unnamed council member's potential conflict of interest or ethics issue.

CONCLUSION: Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), the FPPC cannot address the question or provide analysis unless the requestor is an authorized representative of the council member.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide advice on a conflict of interest matter involving an unnamed council member because the requestor is not an authorized representative of that official.
